title: The Marathons I Ran

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I ran marathons. <break time="350ms"/>Real ones. <break time="300ms"/>Boston twice.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I used to trust my body completely. <break time="300ms"/>Twenty-six point two miles and it just... <break time="250ms"/>did it. <break time="400ms"/>Now some days I can't walk to the mailbox. <break time="350ms"/>I have a medal in a drawer I can't look at. <break time="300ms"/>It feels like it belongs to someone else. <break time="400ms"/>I still grieve her sometimes. <break time="350ms"/>That woman who ran marathons. <break time="300ms"/>That body that cooperated.
story.reflection: <break time="400ms"/>If you're grieving who you used to be - <break time="350ms"/>that's not self-pity. <break time="300ms"/>That's love. <break time="250ms"/>For a version of yourself that deserves to be mourned.

when_to_share: When someone expresses grief about lost abilities or their pre-illness self
